The GhostVLAD approach is a pooling method that was originally proposed for face recognition to improve language identification performance for Indian languages. It is a variant of the VLAD pooling strategy, which is inspired from the VLAD pooling strategy for speaker identification. The GhostVLAD pooling method aggregates the local descriptors into global features by using a modified version of the VLAD pooling strategy. In our case, the local descriptors are features extracted from ResNet, and the global utterance level feature is obtained by using GhostVLAD pooling. The GhostVLAD pooling method outperforms all the other pooling methods by achieving 98.43% F
